source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Amazon ECS services are tagged

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Amazon ECS services are tagged
input_row.description: Ensure all Amazon ECS services have resource tags to facilitate asset management, tracking, and compliance.
input_row.rationale: Consistent tagging supports compliance and helps identify unauthorized or misconfigured resources. Impact: There is minimal administrative overhead associated with implementing and maintaining resource tags.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Under Services, click the name of a service. 5. Click Tags. 6. Ensure at least one tag is listed that does not begin with aws:. Tags prefixed with aws: are AWS-managed. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each ECS cluster and service. From Command Line: Run the following command to list clusters: aws ecs list-clusters Run the following command to list services in a cluster: aws ecs list-services --cluster <cluster-arn> Run the following command to view the tags for a service: aws ecs list-tags-for-resource --resource-arn <service-arn> Ensure that tags are returned that do not begin with aws:. Tags prefixed with aws: are AWS-managed. Repeat for each cluster and service.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Clusters. 3. Click the name of a cluster. 4. Under Services, click the name of a service. 5. Click Tags. 6. Click Manage tags. 7. Click Add tag. 8. Provide a Key and optional Value for the tag. 9. Click Save. 10. Repeat steps 1-9 for each ECS cluster and service requiring remediation. Default Value: By default, Amazon ECS services are not tagged. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/ecs-using- tags.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- clusters.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- services.html 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- tags-for-resource.html
input_row.impact: There is minimal administrative overhead associated with implementing and maintaining resource tags.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

This control is well-suited for automation due to the following reasons:

✓ AWS APIs can query the tags associated with ECS services, using the `list-tags-for-resource` API call to check for tags that do not begin with "aws:".
✓ This is a standard AWS service property related to resource management and tagging, which are commonly exposed via AWS APIs.
✓ The boto3 SDK has methods to retrieve lists of clusters and services, as well as their associated tags, supporting automated audit checks.
✓ The AWS CLI commands provided in the audit section (`aws ecs list-clusters`, `aws ecs list-services`, `aws ecs list-tags-for-resource`) confirm that these properties can be programmatically accessed and verified.
✓ The audit instructions mention AWS CLI commands, indicating that this process can be automated.
✓ The task involves checking for the presence of specific tags, which is a rule-based check that does not require human judgment.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecs_service_tags_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

The automated program would perform the following steps:

1. **Enumerate ECS Clusters:** Use the AWS CLI command `aws ecs list-clusters` or the boto3 method `boto3.client('ecs').list_clusters()` to retrieve a list of ECS cluster ARNs in the account.
2. **Enumerate ECS Services:** For each cluster, use the `aws ecs list-services --cluster <cluster-arn>` command or the `list_services` method to get a list of service ARNs.
3. **Check Tags for Services:**
   - For each service ARN, use the command `aws ecs list-tags-for-resource --resource-arn <service-arn>` or the method `boto3.client('ecs').list_tags_for_resource(ResourceArn=service_arn)` to retrieve associated tags.
   - Validate that at least one tag exists and does not start with "aws:".
4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** Each ECS service has at least one tag that does not begin with "aws:".
   - **Fail:** An ECS service has no tags or only tags that begin with "aws:".
5. **Output Results:** Provide a report of which ECS services meet the tagging criteria and which do not.

The program should be run with sufficient permissions to access ECS resources and list their tags across all relevant regions.
